findingsWithComments.title: CVE-2022-21986
userComments: urn:pwc:pwd:preferredmail:p:2031kushala.rajanna@pwc.com: Approved because the projects SetupMockData and SetupEnvironment affected by this flaw are used only during deployment as a one time solution to set up the environments and seed data and the resources are not accessible by anyone after setting up the product.

urn:pwc:pwd:preferredmail:p:2031jakub.spalek@pwc.com: Technique: M2 :  Establish and maintain control over all of your outputs
Specifics: Components affected by the vulnerability are ASP.NET Core components in the backend of the application. Specifically, these are dependencies of microsoft packages used in SetupMockData and SetupEnvironment projects. These projects are not hosted in any way, they are only used during deployment process to setup the environments configurations and mock data on lower environments. Therefore we believe these do not pose any security threat as they are not accessible by any attack vector.
Remaining Risk: No risk
Verification: Code for SetupMockData and SetupEnvironment projects.

urn:pwc:pwd:preferredmail:p:2031kushala.rajanna@pwc.com: Rejected because if this is a transitive dependency, try utilizing the https://www.npmjs.com/package/npm-force-resolutions package to force the dependency to a vulnerable free version. Additionally, ensure that only production dependencies are included by running npm install --production and including the generated package-lock.json file in the scan.

urn:pwc:pwd:preferredmail:p:2031jakub.spalek@pwc.com: Technique: M2 :  Establish and maintain control over all of your outputs
Specifics: The projects SetupMockData and SetupEnvironment affected by this flaw are used only during deployment as a one time solution to set up the environments and seed data. They are not hosted by any means and therefore are not accessible for any potential attackers.
Remaining Risk: no risk
Verification: Check dependencies and purpose for SetupMockData and SetupEnvironment projects in the codebase.

findingsWithComments.title: SRCCLR-SID-31414
userComments: urn:pwc:pwd:preferredmail:p:2031sayan.basu@pwc.com: Approved because the vulnerable method 'isUnknownOption' is not being used by the library, hence satisfies the mitigation by design.

urn:pwc:pwd:preferredmail:p:2031grzegorz.krempa@pwc.com: Technique: M1 :  Establish and maintain control over all of your inputs
Specifics: We are using @angular/localize package, which uses yargs, which uses yargs-parser. The flaw is in `isUnknownOption` function in `yargs-parser.ts` in package yargs-parser, but the package yargs is not using it, so the flaw does not exists in our scenario.

Link to the flaw description:
https://sca.analysiscenter.veracode.com/vulnerability-database/security/sca/vulnerability/sid-31414/summary

and to the repository:
https://github.com/yargs/yargs
Remaining Risk: none
Verification: local env

urn:pwc:pwd:preferredmail:p:2031grzegorz.krempa@pwc.com: Technique: M1 :  Establish and maintain control over all of your inputs
Specifics: The package yargs-parser is a dependency of dependencies, that we are using. The dependency tree is looking like this:

@angular-devkit/build-angular --> used to build angular application, so it is used only locally and in CI
  webpack-dev-server
    yargs
      yargs-parser

@angular/compiler-cli --> used to build angular application, so it is used only locally and in CI
  yargs
    yargs-parser

karma --> used to run unit tests locally and in CI
  yargs
    yargs-parser

protractor --> used to run UI tests locally and in CI
  yargs
    yargs-parser

stylelint --> used to style the code, so it is used only in local development
  meow
    yargs-parser

As you can see above, that package is not used in production, so can be safely marked as 'ok'.
Remaining Risk: none
Verification: local env

urn:pwc:pwd:preferredmail:p:2031sayan.basu@pwc.com: Rejected because the proposal is not addressing the flaw. We only do review a production ready code without any build or dev dependencies. If this library is a build dependency and not required in production you can use command 'npm install --prod' to get the production ready version and rescan.

urn:pwc:pwd:preferredmail:p:2031grzegorz.krempa@pwc.com: Technique: M1 :  Establish and maintain control over all of your inputs
Specifics: Package "yargs-parser" in version 20.2.7 is used in 2 packages:

yargs (16.2.0) is used here:
   @angular/compiler-cli (10.2.5) --> That package is used for compiling and building application - it is not used in production code
meow (9.0.0) is used here:
   stylelint (13.13.0) --> This package is used to prettify files (scss, css), so it is not used in production.

As you can see that package is not used in production, thus can be safely mitigated.
Remaining Risk: none
Verification: local env

findingsWithComments.title: CVE-2022-34716
userComments: urn:pwc:pwd:preferredmail:p:2031sayan.basu@pwc.com: Approved as reviewed the code the library is a system dependent one only used in build pipeline, it is not present in the production bundle. Hence satisfies the requirements for mitigate by design.

urn:pwc:pwd:preferredmail:p:2031tomasz.mankin@pwc.com: Technique: M3 :  Lock down your environment
Specifics: Checked and confirmed that the artifact does not contain the affected library, checked included libraries on production environment. Confirmed that the packages are used only in the DevOps, hence no direct access is possible while executing DevOps tasks.
Remaining Risk: None
Verification: Source code / AppService (all environments)

urn:pwc:pwd:preferredmail:p:2031sayan.basu@pwc.com: Rejected because the proposal is not addressing the flaw. We only review a scan which is build for production only. Your package should only contain production dependency and anything which is used for build or development purpose should be removed from the package(as it would be done for production environment) and rescanned.

urn:pwc:pwd:preferredmail:p:2031tomasz.mankin@pwc.com: Technique: M3 :  Lock down your environment
Specifics: Listed projects are used only by DevOps agents to setup initial data for the application to use. The are no external inputs (everything is hardcoded) and those projects are not used / running on the production environment.

DevOps agents are isolated, hence no access to them is possible.
Remaining Risk: None
Verification: Source code, application architecture and DevOps.
